commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The branch has narrow fixable defects, but it is maintainer-labeled, member-authored, currently conflicting, and still needs explicit compatibility/security rollout approval.; Needs attention: The intended ClawHub trust hardening is security-positive, but the PR also removes shell quoting from a user-facing QA Docker command.; Review finding: [P2] Restore compose path quoting — extensions/qa-lab/src/docker-ha... (sha=362afb60fb57d0f9a225624b032e2bce8f035c46)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds ClawHub exact-release trust checks for plugin and archive-backed skill installs/updates, acknowledgement CLI plumbing, persisted trust metadata, warning propagation through install/update/doctor/onboarding paths, docs, fixture support, and regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +2084, Tests +3390, Docs +29, Other +21. Total +5524 across 58 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR regression by source inspection: the PR removes current-base QA Docker path quoting and shell quoting that existing tests cover. The ClawHub trust gate itself is security hardening rather than a current-main bug reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The branch has narrow fixable defects, but it is maintainer-labeled, member-authored, currently conflicting, and still needs explicit compatibility/security rollout approval.

target.title: Check ClawHub trust before plugin and skill installs
target.body:
## Summary

OpenClaw now checks ClawHub exact-release trust before downloading ClawHub plugin releases and archive-backed ClawHub skill releases. Malicious or ClawHub-blocked releases fail closed before download; suspicious or failed-review releases are framed as security warnings and require an interactive acknowledgement or `--acknowledge-clawhub-risk`; pending or stale non-risk trust states warn without blocking.

Official/source-linked ClawHub metadata remains a trust signal in package records and UI, but it is not a bypass for the release security gate. For example, `@openclaw/kitchen-sink` is treated as an official/source-linked plugin when ClawHub reports that metadata, but install policy still follows the exact release trust response.

## What Changed

- Shared ClawHub release trust gate: plugin installs and skill archive installs now use the same disposition model for clean, review-recommended, review-required, blocked, stale, and unavailable trust data.
- Plugin installs and updates: preserve the existing plugin behavior from the original trust PR, including typed acknowledgement for risky releases and install-record trust metadata.
- Skill installs and updates: ClawHub archive-backed skills check the selected `slug@version` verdict before archive download; owner-qualified skills validate the returned publisher and show `@owner/slug` in warnings and typed acknowledgements.
- Malicious updates: OpenClaw leaves the installed plugin/skill unchanged, refuses to download the latest malicious release, and now tells the user to uninstall the installed item unless they independently reviewed it.
- Suspicious releases: warnings now say `WARNING - ClawHub found security risks in this release` and describe security findings / blast radius instead of implying the release might be malicious.
- Skill CLI: `openclaw skills install` and `openclaw skills update` now accept `--acknowledge-clawhub-risk` for reviewed non-interactive automation.
- GitHub-backed ClawHub skills: continue to use the ClawHub install resolver as the policy boundary because the skill security-verdict API is version-based, not commit-resolution based.
- Docs: ClawHub and skills CLI docs now describe the trust gate and acknowledgement flag for both plugins and skills.

## Proof

Reviewer-checkable behavior from the local ClawHub fixture command transcript and focused update tests at `362afb60fb57d0f9a225624b032e2bce8f035c46`:

| Scenario | Command Surface | Result |
| --- | --- | --- |
| malicious / blocked plugin release | `openclaw plugins install clawhub:<package>` | exits `1`, prints `BLOCKED`, does not install |
| suspicious plugin release without acknowledgement | `openclaw plugins install clawhub:<package>` | exits `1`, prints `WARNING`, does not install |
| suspicious plugin release with acknowledgement | `openclaw plugins install clawhub:<package> --acknowledge-clawhub-risk` | exits `0`, warning remains visible |
| clean community plugin release | `openclaw plugins install clawhub:<package>` | exits `0` |
| clean official/source-linked plugin release | `openclaw plugins install clawhub:<package>` | exits `0` |
| malicious / blocked skill release | `openclaw skills install @owner/<slug> --global` | exits `1`, prints `BLOCKED`, does not install |
| malicious plugin update | `openclaw plugins update <id>` | keeps the existing install, refuses download, and warns to uninstall unless independently reviewed |
| malicious skill update | `openclaw skills update <slug>` | keeps the existing install, refuses download, and warns to uninstall unless independently reviewed |
| suspicious skill release without acknowledgement | `openclaw skills install @owner/<slug> --global` | exits `1`, prints `WARNING`, does not install |
| suspicious skill release with acknowledgement | `openclaw skills install @owner/<slug> --global --acknowledge-clawhub-risk` | exits `0`, warning remains visible |
| clean community skill release | `openclaw skills install @owner/<slug> --global` | exits `0` |
| clean official/source-linked skill release | `openclaw skills install @owner/<slug> --global` | exits `0` |

The suspicious plugin transcript proves the warning framing and aligned evidence rows:

```text
╭─ WARNING - ClawHub found security risks in this release ───────────────────────╮
│ • Security scan   suspicious                                                   │
│ • Finding         suspicious payload strings                                   │
│                                                                                │
│ This plugin is not marked malicious, but ClawHub found security findings or a  │
│ large local system blast radius.                                               │
│ Review the ClawHub security details before installing.                         │
╰────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

The suspicious skill transcript shows the same policy for skills:

```text
╭─ WARNING - ClawHub found security risks in this release ───────────────────────╮
│ • Security scan   suspicious                                                   │
│ • Finding         security status is not clean                                 │
│                                                                                │
│ This skill is not marked malicious, but ClawHub found security findings or a   │
│ large instruction/tool-use blast radius.                                       │
│ Review the ClawHub security details before installing.                         │
╰────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

The blocked plugin transcript proves the malicious/quarantined field alignment and that raw static-analysis / VirusTotal links are not printed:

```text
╭─ BLOCKED - ClawHub flagged this release as malicious ──────────────────────────╮
│ • Security scan   malicious                                                    │
│ • Moderation      quarantined                                                  │
│ • Finding         manual_moderation                                            │
│ • Scanner         malicious behavior detected                                  │
│ • Finding         Download disabled by ClawHub for this release                │
╰────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

Links:
  Plugin           http://127.0.0.1:<port>/plugins/@community/kitchen-sink
  Security scan    http://127.0.0.1:<port>/plugins/@community/kitchen-sink/security/clawscan
```

The skill transcript also verifies owner-qualified warning links like `/community/kitchen-sink`, so risky owner-qualified releases are not acknowledged as a bare slug. The malicious update rows are covered by focused plugin and skill update tests because the command transcript intentionally exercises install flows end to end.

## Chat / Control UI

- Chat supports `/plugins install ...`; risky ClawHub plugin installs cannot be acknowledged from chat and tell the user to use the local `openclaw plugins install ... --acknowledge-clawhub-risk` command from a trusted shell.
- Chat supports `/skill <name> [input]` for invoking existing skills, but there is no `/skills install` chat command path in this PR.
- Control UI does have a Skills panel ClawHub install path. It calls the Gateway `skills.install` method with `{ source: "clawhub", slug }`; malicious/suspicious skill releases fail before download there too, and suspicious releases surface as the method error rather than an interactive terminal acknowledgement box.

## Screenshots

Terminal screenshots are not embedded because the PR proof upload helper is blocked in this environment. `github_screenshot_upload.sh --check-only --repo openclaw/openclaw` fails because `gh-image` has no readable GitHub web upload session and `GH_SESSION_TOKEN` is not set for this process. The command transcript above is the current reviewer-visible proof until screenshots can be uploaded through a supported GitHub browser session or local session token.

## Verification

Focused local checks on `362afb60fb57d0f9a225624b032e2bce8f035c46`:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/clawhub.test.ts src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.test.ts` passed: 2 shards, 130 tests.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/update-cli.test.ts src/plugins/update.test.ts src/commands/onboarding-plugin-install.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/missing-configured-plugin-install.test.ts` passed: 4 shards, 374 tests.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/commands-plugins.install.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/skills.clawhub.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/skills.test.ts` passed: 3 shards, 57 tests.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/infra/clawhub-install-trust.ts src/commands/onboarding-plugin-install.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/missing-configured-plugin-install.ts src/cli/update-cli/update-command.ts src/plugins/clawhub.test.ts src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.test.ts src/plugins/update.test.ts src/cli/update-cli.test.ts src/commands/onboarding-plugin-install.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/missing-configured-plugin-install.test.ts` passed.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/infra/clawhub-install-trust.ts src/commands/onboarding-plugin-install.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/missing-configured-plugin-install.ts src/cli/update-cli/update-command.ts src/plugins/clawhub.test.ts src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.test.ts src/plugins/update.test.ts src/cli/update-cli.test.ts src/commands/onboarding-plugin-install.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/missing-configured-plugin-install.test.ts` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- `node .artifacts/clawhub-install-trust/run-proof.cjs` passed and regenerated the ten-scenario plugin/skill trust command transcript.

Not run locally: full `pnpm check` / full build. The PR CI is running those broader gates on the pushed head.
target.author: jesse-merhi
